opp.next_step: May 14: Dear Business Partner,Thank you for your participation in our rapid RFI process, as well as for the time and effort invested in your demonstration sessions and detailed responses. We would like to provide you with an update on our current progress and next steps.At present, our internal team is consolidating and reviewing all submissions, including both functional capabilities and commercial aspects. This phase is focused on developing a comprehensive recommendation for our business stakeholders. Following this, the business will review the findings and determine a final recommendation. This outcome will then be submitted to the Executive Committee for approval, or for the identification of any additional requirements that may need to be addressed.Given that we are still within the evaluation and internal governance stages, we are unfortunately not able to provide definitive feedback or outcomes at this time. We also expect that timelines may vary, particularly as the process progresses through executive review. We appreciate your understanding and patience while we complete this process. We will keep you informed of any material updates and will revert as soon as we are able to provide more definitive feedback or next steps.In the interim, should we require any further clarification or information from your side, we will reach out directly.Thank you once again for your engagement and cooperation throughout this process. opp.ais_why:
• Sept 2025 Avoma notes capture Zycus pitching the 'Embedded GenAI platform (Merlin) across source-to-pay modules and Teams connectivity for request orchestration' to De Beers' Patricia Smith, plus the Anglo American Zycus footprint as a peer reference. Merlin and the full S2C+SRM suite are in scope (iContract, iRisk, iSave, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin). The Merlin-across-modules architecture is the rep's positioning anchor.
• Formal Evaluation stage, BD Engaged; De Beers' planned Jan 2027 demerger from Anglo American creates a real buying-window catalyst as De Beers needs its own standalone tech stack. RFI is expected; multiple Zycus stakeholders (Menachem Harari, Nishita Sareen, Caroline Lacocque, Parleen Kour, Asmita Mukherjee) and a broad De Beers committee have remained active through 2025-2026.
• High confidence: SF Description was null but Avoma provides clear AI/Merlin pitch sentiment and a tangible Jan 2027 demerger catalyst. Held at 8 (not 9-10) because the customer's emphasis in notes has been cost/budget under the standalone entity rather than an explicit 'AI-first selection criterion'. Anglo American reference + Merlin-cross-module positioning anchors AI Hungry.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated a clear business driver: De Beers is undergoing a demerger from the relevant stakeholder (effective Jan 2027, now ~6 months out) and existing procurement contracts end in 2027, creating a forcing function to establish standalone sourcing and procurement systems. On the Sep 30, 2025 discovery call, Patricia Smith (Category Manager) stated the demerger constraint multiple times, emphasizing cost discipline as the defining priority for the smaller standalone entity: 'we're a smaller entity now; we don't have that Anglo American deep wallet; costing is very important.' However, no quantified savings target, cycle-time improvement, compliance metric, or ROI has been articulated by the buyer or co-developed by the relevant stakeholder was shared Apr 30, but no TCO model, ROI workshop, or value-case validation has been logged, and no feedback on cost fit has been received in the 62 days since. The business problem is confirmed (demerger readiness, contract renewal, cost control), but the quantified business case required to secure Economic Buyer and executive approval is missing. This is a critical gap: in a competitive RFP where cost is the stated top priority, the vendor who quantifies and co-owns the value case with the buyer has a structural advantage.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Patricia Smith (Category Manager, Supply Chain) has been the primary and near-exclusive contact throughout the RFP process (discovery call Sep 30, 2025, demo coordination Apr 23, 2026, pricing receipt Apr 30), but she has shown no evidence of internal advocacy, multi-threading, or power access. On the Sep 30 call, Patricia explicitly positioned herself as a process coordinator, not a decision-maker: she deferred budget authority to an unnamed executive layer and stated the evaluation outcome would be submitted to the Executive Committee. The May 14 Next Step message was a formal, arms-length holding communication with no forward momentum, no champion urgency, and no Zycus differentiation. Patricia has not introduced Zycus to senior stakeholders (Zungu Ntobeko Group Category Manager, Pierre Joubert Director IT, or the Economic Buyer/Executive Committee members), has not shared competitive intelligence, has not driven the internal recommendation process, and has not responded to pricing or provided feedback in 62 days. This is the behavior of a neutral process administrator, not a champion. The deal is single-threaded to a role with no power and no advocacy. In a competitive RFP where a rival may have secured a champion who is multi-threading to the Economic Buyer, driving the internal scorecard, and positioning their vendor as the demerger-readiness solution, Zycus is structurally disadvantaged. Building or identifying a champion (or escalating to senior buyer stakeholders who can champion) is the highest-priority action.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitor is logged in SF Competitors__c or any related synthesis-map field (Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c). The May 14 Next Step message states the buyer is 'consolidating and reviewing all submissions' (plural), confirming a multi-vendor RFP, but the number of competing vendors, their identities, and Zycus's standing relative to them are unknown. The 48-day silence post-pricing and the lack of any shortlist feedback, reference requests, ROI workshop, or executive engagement could signal several competitive scenarios: (1) Zycus is not shortlisted and the buyer is advancing with other vendors, (2) Zycus is shortlisted but another vendor is the front-runner and the buyer is completing due diligence on that vendor first, (3) the evaluation is genuinely stalled due to internal delays or demerger complexity, with all vendors waiting, or (4) a competitor has secured a champion, multi-threaded to power, and is driving the internal decision while Zycus waits passively. Without competitive intelligence, Zycus cannot position differentiation, cannot counter competitor narratives, and cannot assess win probability. In a large enterprise RFP in the Source-to-Pay space, likely competitors include SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, and Procurement-as-a-Service providers. The vendor who has visibility into the competitive landscape (through a champion or direct buyer feedback) and has positioned a clear wedge (e.g. Merlin AI for demerger-speed advantage, SPRM depth, cost-effective phased deployment) will control the outcome. Operating blind is a critical risk.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and acute: De Beers is undergoing a demerger from the relevant stakeholder (effective Jan 2027, now ~6 months out) and must establish standalone procurement and sourcing systems before existing contracts expire in 2027. On the Sep 30, 2025 discovery call, Patricia Smith (Category Manager, Supply Chain) articulated this forcing function multiple times and emphasized the financial constraint it creates: 'we're a smaller entity now; we don't have that Anglo American deep wallet; costing is very important.' The pain is both operational (need to stand up procurement infrastructure for the standalone entity) and financial (cost discipline as the defining constraint post-demerger). The pain owner is the Supply Chain organization (represented by Patricia Smith, Laren Perumal Senior Manager Supply Chain, and the 14-person contact roster), but the ultimate pain owner is the executive leadership responsible for demerger readiness and the budget authority layer (Executive Committee) who must approve the investment. The pain is time-bound (demerger Jan 2027, contracts ending 2027) and mission-critical, which creates urgency and a compelling event. However, the pain has not been quantified (no savings target, no risk-mitigation metric, no compliance or cycle-time goal), and Zycus has not co-developed a business case that ties the solution to the pain in measurable terms. In a competitive RFP, the vendor who quantifies the pain, co-owns the solution with the pain owner, and ties the business case to the demerger forcing function will be best positioned to win executive approval.

paper_process.narrative: No visibility into contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel) or implementation scoping. The only paper-process signal is an NDA: the Nov 27, 2025 Next Step mentions 'DeBeers send a few new questions which is currently being evaluated by pre-sales,' and prior sweep history records an open commitment for Zycus to expedite NDA legal review and send the reviewed NDA to Patricia Smith, and for the buyer to finalize and send the RFI document after receiving the signed NDA. However, there is no evidence the NDA was executed, and no subsequent discussion of legal, InfoSec, or contracting has been logged. The products in scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk) represent a significant Source-to-Pay platform deployment that will require ERP integration, data migration, user training, and change management, but no implementation partner, SI engagement, or technical deep-dive has been mentioned. The close date (Sep 30, 2026, 91 days out) implies contracting, InfoSec review, and implementation kickoff must begin within weeks, but none of these workstreams are visible or in motion. In a demerger context where the buyer is establishing a standalone entity with ending 2027 contracts as a forcing function, procurement and legal processes may be complex and time-consuming, and the vendor who has proactively scoped and de-risked the paper process (through early legal, InfoSec, and implementation engagement) will have a faster path to close. This is a critical blind spot.

The May 14 Next Step message reiterated this: the internal team's recommendation will go to 'the business' for review, then to 'the Executive Committee for approval.' No one from that executive layer has engaged with the relevant stakeholder 14 Opportunity Contact Roles include two senior titles who may hold budget authority: Zungu Ntobeko (Group Category Manager) and Pierre Joubert (Director - IT Advisory and Management Services), but neither has participated in a logged call, meeting, or activity. decision_process.narrative: The buyer has articulated a three-stage decision process: (1) internal team consolidation and evaluation of all vendor submissions (functional and commercial), (2) business stakeholder review and final recommendation, (3) Executive Committee approval. This was stated explicitly in the May 14 Next Step message and aligns with Patricia Smith's Sep 30 comment that the outcome would be submitted to the Executive Committee. The process is formal and multi-layered, typical of a large enterprise procurement in the context of a corporate demerger. However, critical process details are unknown: the size of the shortlist, the number of competing vendors, the timeline for each stage (May 14 message warned 'timelines may vary, particularly as the process progresses through executive review'), the identity of the business stakeholders and Executive Committee members who will make the final call, and whether Zycus has a voice or champion in any of those review layers. The 48-day silence since May 14 suggests the process is either stalled in stage 1 (internal consolidation), stage 2 (business review), or stage 3 (executive approval), but Zycus has no visibility. The call sequence shows only one discovery call (Sep 30, 2025), one demo (Apr 23, 2026), and no follow-up executive, commercial, or validation sessions, indicating the process is running entirely on the buyer's side with no Zycus involvement post-pricing. In a competitive RFP, the vendor who is actively engaged in the evaluation stages (through a champion driving internal advocacy or direct access to the business/executive review layers) can shape the outcome; a vendor waiting passively for the buyer's decision is at risk.

However, the specific decision criteria, scoring model, weighting, and shortlist mechanics have not been shared with the relevant stakeholder products in scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk) indicate a broad Source-to-Pay platform evaluation, but whether the buyer prioritizes SPRM depth, AI/Merlin capabilities, integration ease, demerger-readiness timeline, or total cost of ownership is unknown. deltas.subject: Cost-discipline wedge undeveloped: Patricia emphasized three times on the 30 Sep call that budget constraint is the demerger's defining challenge ('we're a smaller entity now; we don't have that Anglo American deep wallet; costing is very important'). Zycus shared pricing 30 Apr but has not co-developed a TCO model, a demerger-scenario ROI case, or a phased-deployment cost plan that addresses her stated pain. A competitor who has quantified the savings (e.g. contract-leakage prevention, supplier-consolidation ROI, faster cycle time) and tied it to the Jan 2027 breakaway timeline has a stronger value narrative. This is the highest-leverage near-term wedge: position Zycus as the cost-effective, rapid-deployment, demerger-ready solution vs a heavyweight enterprise platform that requires deep pockets and long implementation. Immediate move: prepare a De Beers demerger ROI workshop (1-2 hours, virtual, invite Patricia + Zungu + finance stakeholder) to co-build a savings model anchored to their contract-expiration forcing function and the standalone-entity budget reality; use it to re-engage and multi-thread in one move.
